locations.commerce: Import: Foodstuffs, Industrial Goods, Electronic Goods. - Export: Commercial Goods, Advanced Technology
locations.description: Background: Stardust City (or just “the City”, to locals) is a gleaming beacon of progress. It was originally the crown city of the Stardust Empire, and became the hometown of Stardust, Inc. when it was founded. A sprawling metropolis running entirely on Crystal power, Stardust City has been the seat of power of Stardust, Inc. since its founding, and it makes sure no one forgets it. From food to sanitation, communications and construction, everything is owned and run by Stardust, Inc.. THORN also has a significant presence here, as the best place to fight is in the core of the enemy’s territory. Landscape: The first things anyone thinks of when Stardust CIty is mentioned are the gleaming skyscrapers and organised streets. The centre of the city is laid out in a neat grid, making it very easy to navigate. The city also boasts the most recognisable skyline in the world, with the cluster of towering skyscrapers at its heart dwarfed by the ring of Crystal power generators spaced around the city’s original boundary. As time passed, the city grew, but the construction standards were not maintained beyond the ring of power generators. The result is that, rather than being akin to suburbs, the outer areas resemble slums. These areas house people with just enough worth to Stardust that they are allowed to remain, commuting to work inside the city proper. The night in Stardust City glows a cyan colour with the light pollution from the huge number of Crystals housed within the great power plants, making the city seem to glow as if it is made of stardust. Population: Stardust, Inc. does not allow professional discrimination, so all races can be found alongside each other in its home city. There is, however, a slight imbalance toward the faster-reproducing Sen, which is almost inevitable over a period of time. As the corporation owns the city, all available jobs relate to it in some way, whether it’s working as accountants, engineers or researchers for Stardust, Inc. directly, or providing services to employees, such as shopkeepers and couriers. Laws: The laws of Stardust City have been revised from what was in place when Stardust was an empire, with most being lifted directly out of the corporation’s handbook rather than designed for the people. In addition to “common sense” laws like those relating to violence and theft, there are surprisingly large penalties relating to day-to-day conduct and presentation. Vandalism, public disorder and similar infractions are viewed as serious crimes by Stardust, Inc., as it is very focused on its image. It therefore punishes them more severely than most places in the world. The laws are enforced by the Stardust robots which roam the streets. Although they are officially overseen by Warrior-Monks, these figureheads have no real say in how justice is dispensed. Instead, the Peacekeeping Division of Stardust, Inc. makes all of the decisions. Dissenters say that the number of robots used to police the city is excessive – tens of villages could be protected if the robot presence at the centre were reduced. Stardust, Inc. laims that, for as long as groups like THORN subvert the law, the level of protection provided by the robots is needed to keep the population safe.

locations.commerce: Import: Commercial Goods, Foodstuffs, Electronic Goods. - Export: Industrial Goods, Natural Raw Materials
locations.description: Background: Sekkei has been an ever-present ally of the Stardust Empire since before the last Great War and has therefore been allowed to thrive, growing into the only other major city on the Centra continent. It made its substantial wealth as an industrial hub by mining the nearby Tekka Mountains and harvesting the forests that surround it to provide Stardust, Inc. with raw materials. Sekkei is valued highly by the corporation for its contribution to the continuous march of technological progress. Sekkei is, however, a slave to rampant corruption in its political system: the officials get rich and powerful based off the work of honest citizens. Landscape: Located near the southern coast of Centra, Sekkei enjoys a tropical climate with abundant plant life in the surrounding area. Sekkei owns the verdant forests surrounding it; its territory reaches as far as the Tekka Mountains. The city’s tradition is to hide any structure or technology that is visually at odds with nature, even going as far as allowing ivy and vines to snake up all of their buildings. They generally forgo public screens and rely on printed media, or even public speakers in some districts, to share news. The larger trappings of modern technology, which cannot be concealed by plants, tend to be hidden underground, including factories and the city’s Crystal power plants. As a result, the winding, cobbled streets make the city feel more like a quaint town than one of the major cities of the world, although the population density doesn’t reflect that during the daytime. The single exception to the rule is the Hall of Justice, the seat of the Protectorate (and, nominally, the Warrior-Monks for the region). This former defensive fortress dominates the skyline, its solid stone profile casting a shadow across the city. It is always lit, and is said to provide a beacon of hope to those who live within the city. Population: Sekkei has always accepted anyone who wanted to live there and was willing to work, so the majority of the population are a specific mix of hard workers who are proud of their heritage and would do anything for their home. The mix is slightly heavier towards the Sen, as the city was originally founded by their race, but there are many of each race to be found in Sekkei. Most of the available jobs are either trading or manual labour – mining, felling trees and such – but Sekkei is also home to scientists and support staff who work with Stardust, Inc.. Laws: The laws in Sekkei are made by the Protectorate, though few laws have been passed in recent years. As the government becomes increasingly corrupt, they prefer to reduce the restrictions on themselves and the population, rather than increase them. Whatever laws exist are enforced by Stardust robots, which are specially reprogrammed to only enforce the laws present within Sekkei. The Warrior-Monks also have a small contingent in the city, some of whom patrol the streets each day, following their centuries-old tradition. While the city’s leaders are trying to remove them, they have had little success against the weight of public opinion: officially, the Warrior-Monks have the final say regarding law and order… although they do not often exercise it.
